impossibility_statement: Any continuous self-map of a compact convex set must have a fixed point; it is impossible to continuously retract a disk onto its boundary
formal_source: L.E.J. Brouwer, 1911. Uber Abbildung von Mannigfaltigkeiten. Mathematische Annalen 71(1):97-115
why_closure_fails: The identity map on the boundary has nontrivial degree; extending it to a retraction would collapse homology, contradicting functoriality of H_n
key_references: Milnor 1978 Analytic proofs of the hairy ball theorem and the Brouwer fixed-point theorem

impossibility_statement: Any upper semicontinuous set-valued map from a compact convex set to itself with convex values must have a fixed point; no escape is possible under these conditions
formal_source: Shizuo Kakutani, 1941. A generalization of Brouwer's fixed point theorem. Duke Mathematical Journal 8(3):457-459
why_closure_fails: Approximate selections via Michael selection theorem reduce to Brouwer; convexity of values prevents escape through averaging
key_references: Nash 1950 (uses Kakutani for Nash equilibrium existence)

impossibility_statement: A continuous map lifts to a covering space if and only if its induced fundamental group homomorphism lands in the covering subgroup; otherwise lifting is impossible
formal_source: Lifting criterion theorem, formalized by Seifert and Threlfall 1934; modern treatment in Hatcher Ch.1
why_closure_fails: The long exact sequence of homotopy groups for the fibration forces pi_1 compatibility; loops in the base that are not in the covering subgroup cannot be unwound

impossibility_statement: No single computable knot invariant can distinguish all non-equivalent knots; every known invariant has blind spots where distinct knots receive identical values
formal_source: Multiple results: Jones polynomial fails to distinguish mutant knots (Morton 1986); unknot detection via Jones polynomial is open (Kronheimer-Mrowka 2011 partial); HOMFLY-PT has coincidences
why_closure_fails: Each invariant captures only partial topological information; mutation operations preserve many polynomial invariants while changing the knot type

impossibility_statement: An n-dimensional manifold cannot always be embedded in R^(2n-1); dimension 2n is necessary in general. Characteristic class obstructions prevent lower-dimensional embeddings
formal_source: Hassler Whitney, 1936. Differentiable manifolds. Annals of Mathematics 37(3):645-680; Whitney 1944 self-intersection formula
why_closure_fails: Stiefel-Whitney classes provide mod-2 obstructions; the normal bundle must admit sections, and characteristic class non-vanishing blocks dimension reduction

impossibility_statement: Not every space satisfying Poincare duality is a manifold; surgery theory obstructions (Wall groups) prevent realization of Poincare duality spaces as manifolds
formal_source: C.T.C. Wall, 1970. Surgery on Compact Manifolds. London Mathematical Society Monographs
why_closure_fails: The surgery exact sequence contains L-group obstructions; the signature and Arf invariant provide secondary obstructions that cannot be eliminated by homotopy equivalence alone

impossibility_statement: A compact manifold admits a nowhere-vanishing vector field if and only if its Euler characteristic is zero; nonzero chi forces singularities
formal_source: Poincare 1885, Hopf 1926. Vektorfelder in n-dimensionalen Mannigfaltigkeiten. Mathematische Annalen 96:225-249
why_closure_fails: Poincare-Hopf theorem forces the sum of indices of zeroes to equal chi(M); when chi != 0, at least one singularity must exist. Generalizes hairy ball to all dimensions and topologies
key_references: Milnor 1965 Topology from the Differentiable Viewpoint

impossibility_statement: Finite-volume hyperbolic manifolds of dimension >= 3 have no continuous deformations; the geometry is completely determined by the fundamental group. Moduli space is a single point
formal_source: G. Daniel Mostow, 1968. Quasi-conformal mappings in n-space and the rigidity of hyperbolic space forms. Publications Mathematiques de l'IHES 34:53-104
why_closure_fails: In dim >= 3, quasi-conformal maps between hyperbolic manifolds must be conformal (isometric). The boundary extension argument via ergodic theory on the sphere at infinity forces rigidity

impossibility_statement: The integral of Gaussian curvature over a closed surface is locked to 2*pi*chi; it is impossible to change total curvature without changing topology
formal_source: Gauss 1827 Disquisitiones Generales; Bonnet 1848; Chern 1944 (higher dimensions). Allendoerfer-Weil 1943 for general case
why_closure_fails: Gauss-Bonnet is a topological invariant computed from geometry; the integral is insensitive to local metric deformations, locking global curvature to discrete topological data

impossibility_statement: C^1 isometric embeddings are flexible (Nash-Kuiper: can embed flat torus in R^3) but C^2 embeddings are rigid (must respect curvature). Smoothness and geometry cannot be simultaneously free
formal_source: Nash 1954 C^1 isometric embeddings; Nash 1956 smooth case. Kuiper 1955. Borisov 2004 fractional regularity threshold
structural_pattern: MAP(embed_isometrically) -> COMPLETE(smooth+isometric) FAILS at C^1/C^2 boundary -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(regularity)
why_closure_fails: Below C^2 the convex integration method allows wild solutions (h-principle); at C^2 and above, curvature constraints from Gauss equations become rigid PDE obstructions

impossibility_statement: R^4 is the unique Euclidean space admitting uncountably many exotic smooth structures; classification of smooth 4-manifolds is algorithmically undecidable. Smooth = topological fails uniquely in dimension 4
formal_source: Donaldson 1983 (gauge theory obstructions); Freedman 1982 (topological classification); Taubes 1987 (exotic R^4 existence)
why_closure_fails: Donaldson's theorem shows the intersection form constrains smooth structures via Yang-Mills instantons; Freedman's theorem shows topological freedom, creating an irreducible gap

impossibility_statement: Every closed orientable 3-manifold arises from surgery on a link in S^3, but the surgery coefficients are constrained by linking matrix signature; not all coefficient choices yield distinct manifolds
formal_source: Lickorish 1962 A representation of orientable combinatorial 3-manifolds; Wallace 1960; Kirby 1978 calculus of framed links
why_closure_fails: Kirby moves generate equivalences between surgery presentations; the resulting quotient is the set of 3-manifolds, but the equivalence relation is computationally intractable
key_references: Saveliev 1999 Lectures on the Topology of 3-Manifolds

impossibility_statement: There exist oracles A and B such that P^A = NP^A and P^B != NP^B; therefore no relativizing proof technique can resolve P vs NP
formal_source: Baker, Gill, Solovay 1975. Relativizations of the P=?NP question. SIAM Journal on Computing 4(4):431-442
structural_pattern: MAP(proof_technique) -> COMPLETE(P_vs_NP) FAILS if RELATIVIZING -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(oracle_dependent)
why_closure_fails: Relativizing arguments treat the oracle as a black box; since both outcomes are realizable by appropriate oracles, any proof resolving P vs NP must exploit internal structure of computation

impossibility_statement: If one-way functions exist, then no 'natural' proof strategy (constructive, large, useful) can prove super-polynomial circuit lower bounds against P/poly
formal_source: Razborov, Rudich 1997. Natural proofs. Journal of Computer and System Sciences 55(1):24-35
why_closure_fails: A natural proof provides a polynomial-time computable property that distinguishes hard functions from random; but pseudorandom generators (from OWFs) make hard functions look random, defeating any such distinguisher
key_references: Carmosino et al. 2016 (NEXP lower bounds from derandomized natural proofs)

impossibility_statement: There exist algebraic oracles relative to which both P=NP and P!=NP hold; therefore no algebrizing proof technique can resolve P vs NP or most major complexity separations
formal_source: Aaronson, Wigderson 2009. Algebrization: A new barrier in complexity theory. ACM Transactions on Computation Theory 1(1):1-54
why_closure_fails: Algebrizing techniques extend oracle queries to low-degree polynomial extensions; the arithmetization trick used in IP=PSPACE algebrizes, but both outcomes remain consistent with algebraic oracles

impossibility_statement: NP = PCP(log n, 1): every NP proof can be verified by reading only a constant number of random bits. Consequence: for many NP-hard optimization problems, even approximating within a constant factor is NP-hard
formal_source: Arora, Lund, Motwani, Sudan, Szegedy 1998. Proof verification and the hardness of approximation problems. Journal of the ACM 45(3):501-555. Dinur 2007 combinatorial proof
structural_pattern: MAP(approximate) -> REDUCE(error_ratio) FAILS below threshold -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(proof_structure)
why_closure_fails: The PCP characterization of NP means that gap amplification makes distinguishing satisfiable from far-from-satisfiable as hard as exact satisfiability; approximation inherits worst-case hardness

impossibility_statement: Computing certain functions (disjointness, inner product) requires linear communication between two parties regardless of protocol; no clever encoding can compress the necessary information exchange below Omega(n) bits
formal_source: Yao 1979. Some complexity questions related to distributive computing. STOC 1979. Kalyanasundaram-Schnitger 1992 (disjointness). Razborov 1992 (distributional complexity of disjointness)
why_closure_fails: Information-theoretic arguments show that computing set disjointness requires each party to reveal Omega(n) bits of information about their input; compression below this threshold loses essential correlations

impossibility_statement: The MOD_q function cannot be computed by constant-depth circuits with AND/OR/MOD_p gates for distinct primes p,q; certain explicit functions require super-polynomial size bounded-depth circuits
formal_source: Razborov 1987 (AC^0 lower bounds for clique); Smolensky 1987 (algebraic methods); Hastad 1986 (exponential lower bounds for constant-depth circuits, switching lemma)
why_closure_fails: Low-degree polynomials over F_p cannot approximate MOD_q for p != q; the algebraic structure of the gate basis is incompatible with the target function's algebraic structure over a different characteristic

impossibility_statement: For problems like sorting and element distinctness, any algorithm must satisfy T * S >= Omega(n^2) where T is time and S is space; reducing one forces increase in the other
formal_source: Borodin-Cook 1982 A time-space tradeoff for sorting on a general sequential model of computation. SIAM J. Computing 11(2):287-297. Beame 1991
why_closure_fails: Information bottleneck: with limited space, intermediate results must be recomputed; the product lower bound arises from counting argument on branching program width vs length

impossibility_statement: If the Unique Games Conjecture holds, optimal inapproximability thresholds for Max-Cut, Vertex Cover, and many CSPs are exactly characterized by SDP relaxation gaps; beating the SDP bound is NP-hard
formal_source: Khot 2002. On the power of unique 2-prover 1-round games. STOC 2002. Khot-Kindler-Mossel-O'Donnell 2007 (optimal Max-Cut hardness)
why_closure_fails: Unique Games hardness reductions show that the SDP relaxation captures all polynomial-time extractable information; the integrality gap between SDP and integer program is computationally irreducible

impossibility_statement: Any language decidable in polynomial space has an interactive proof with a polynomial-time verifier; conversely, interactive proofs cannot go beyond PSPACE. Interaction + randomness exactly capture space
formal_source: Shamir 1992. IP = PSPACE. Journal of the ACM 39(4):869-877. Lund-Fortnow-Karloff-Nisan 1992
structural_pattern: EXTEND(deterministic->interactive) -> COMPLETE(verification_power) SATURATES at PSPACE -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(arithmetization)
why_closure_fails: Arithmetization converts Boolean formulas to polynomial evaluations over large fields; the verifier can check polynomial identity via Schwartz-Zippel, but the polynomial degree bounds the complexity to PSPACE

hub_name: Minimum Circuit Size Problem (MCSP) Hardness Barrier
impossibility_statement: Proving MCSP is NP-hard under standard (Karp) reductions would imply breakthrough circuit lower bounds (E not in P/poly); the meta-problem of circuit minimization resists classification by known techniques
formal_source: Kabanets-Cai 2000. Circuit minimization problem. STOC 2000. Allender-Das 2014. Murray-Williams 2017
structural_pattern: MAP(reduce_to_MCSP) -> COMPLETE(NP_hardness) FAILS without circuit lower bounds -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(self_reference)
why_closure_fails: Proving MCSP NP-hard via Karp reductions would provide an efficient way to distinguish hard truth tables from easy ones, which is itself a circuit lower bound; the problem is self-referentially trapped
